cable_car.description: A cable car run. Just one or two large cars. The traction cable forms a loop, but the cars do not loop around, they just move up and down on their own side, rolling along static cables over which they are suspended.

gondola.description: An aerialway where the cabins go around in a circle.

mixed_lift.description: A mixed lift, containing both gondolas and chairs.

chair_lift.description: An open chairlift run. These have one or more seats or benches and are open to the outside air.

drag_lift.description: An overhead tow-line for skiers and riders.

zip_line.description: Zip line, gravity propelled aerial ropeslide like a flying fox or similar

goods.description: A cable supported lift for conveying goods, not passengers.

college.description: Campus or buildings of an institute of Further Education (aka continuing education)

school.description: School and grounds - primary, middle and secondary schools

university.description: A university campus: an institute of higher education

community_centre.description: A place mostly used for local events, festivities and group activities; including special interest and special age groups. .

administrative.description: An administrative boundary. Subdivisions of areas/territories/jurisdictions recognised by governments or other organisations for administrative purposes. These range from large groups of nation states right down to small administrative districts and suburbs, as indicated by the

census.description: A census-designated boundary delineating a statistical area, not necessarily observable on the ground.

forest.description: A delimited forest is a land which is predominantly wooded and which is, for this reason, given defined boundaries. It may cover different tree stands, non-wooded areas, highways… but all the area within the boundaries are considered and managed as a single forest.

forest_compartment.description: A forest compartment is a numbered sub-division within a delimited forest, physically materialized with visible, typically cleared, boundaries.

health.description: Health division boundaries. Some mappers use

limited_traffic_zone.description: Describes an officially designated area that requires authorization for the entry of certain vehicles.

local_authority.description: Describes the territory of a local authority.

low_emission_zone.description: A geographically defined area which seeks to restrict or deter access by certain polluting vehicles with the aim of improving the air quality.

national_park.description: Area of outstanding natural beauty, set aside for conservation and for recreation (

postal_code.description: Postal code boundaries. Some mappers use

special_economic_zone.description: A government-defined area in which business and trade laws are different.

statistical.description: An official boundary recognised by government for statistical purposes.

disputed.description: An area of landed claimed by two or more parties (use with caution). See also

public_transport.description: An operating area of a transit authority.

assembly_point.description: A designated (safe) place where people can gather or must report to during an emergency or a fire drill.

footway.description: For designated footpaths; i.e., mainly/exclusively for pedestrians. This includes walking tracks and gravel paths. If bicycles are allowed as well, you can indicate this by adding a

bridleway.description: For horse riders. Pedestrians are usually also permitted, cyclists may be permitted depending on local rules/laws. Motor vehicles are forbidden.

education.description: An area predominately used for educational purposes/facilities.

farmland.description: An area of farmland used for tillage (cereals, vegetables, oil plants, flowers).

logging.description: An area where some or all trees have been cut down

meadow.description: A meadow or pasture: land primarily vegetated by grass and non-woody plants, mainly used for hay or grazing

orchard.description: intentional planting of trees or shrubs maintained for food production

grass.description: An area of mown and managed grass not otherwise covered by a more specific tag. Some view this as not a landuse, see the main page

greenfield.description: Describes land scheduled for new development where there have been no buildings before. A greenfield is scheduled to turn into a construction site

recreation_ground.description: An open green space for general recreation, which may include pitches, nets and so on, usually municipal but possibly also private to colleges or companies

village_green.description: A village green is a distinctive area of grassy public land in a village centre. Not a generic tag for urban greenery. It is a typical English term – defined separately from 'common land' under the Commons Registration Act 1965 and the Commons Act 2006.

greenery.description: Used for any area covered with landscaping or decorative greenery, regardless of it being on a roundabout, along a street or in a park/garden, This tag has been used for vegetation that is hard to classify, either because its kind or because its diversity.

winter_sports.description: An area dedicated to winter sports (e.g. skiing)

firepit.description: A fire ring or fire pit, often at a campsite or picnic site.

garden.description: A place where flowers and other plants are grown in a decorative and structured manner or for scientific purposes.

nature_reserve.description: A protected area of importance for wildlife, flora, fauna or features of geological or other special interest.

park.description: A park, usually in an urban (municipal) setting, created for recreation and relaxation.

summer_camp.description: A place for supervised camps for children or teenagers conducted during the summer months.

fell.description: Habitat above tree line covered with grass, dwarf shrubs and mosses.

grassland.description: An area where the vegetation is dominated by grasses and other herbaceous (non-woody) plants.

tundra.description: Treeless cold climate habitat with open, low growing sedges, grasses, mosses and lichens.

bay.description: Named area of water mostly surrounded by land but with level connection to the ocean or a lake.

beach.description: Landform along a body of water which consists of sand, shingle or other loose material.

cape.description: A piece of elevated land sticking out into the sea or large lake.

coastline.description: The line between sea and land indicating mean high water (springs): the water lies on the right side of the way.

crevasse.description: A large crack in a glacier

glacier.description: A permanent body of ice formed naturally from snow that is moving under its own weight.

hot_spring.description: A spring of geothermally heated groundwater

isthmus.description: A narrow strip of land, bordered by water on both sides and connecting two larger land masses

mud.description: Area covered with mud: water saturated fine grained soil without significant plant growth.

peninsula.description: A piece of land projecting into water from a larger land mass, nearly surrounded by water

reef.description: A feature (rock, sandbar, coral, etc) lying permanently beneath the surface of the water.

shingle.description: An accumulation of rounded rock fragments on a beach or riverbed.

shoal.description: An area of the water floor which nears the water surface and is exposed at low tide or when a river/lake is not full of water.

spring.description: A place where ground water flows naturally from the ground

strait.description: A narrow area of water surrounded by land on two sides and by water on two other sides.

water.description: Any inland body of water, from natural such as a lake or pond to artificial like a moat or canal.

wetland.description: A natural area subject to inundation or with waterlogged ground

cliff.description: A vertical or almost vertical natural drop in terrain, usually with a bare rock surface. The bottom of the cliff is on the right side of the way.

dune.description: A hill of sand formed by wind, covered with no or very little vegetation

hill.description: A hill is a landform that is elevated above the surrounding terrain, but is smaller than a mountain.

saddle.description: The lowest point along a ridge or between two mountain tops.

sand.description: An area covered by sand with no or very little vegetation.

sinkhole.description: A natural depression or hole in the surface topography.

valley.description: A natural depression flanked by ridges or ranges of mountains or hills

country.description: A nation state or other high-level national political/administrative area.

region.description: Used both as a broad tag for geographic or historical areas with no clear boundary and for distinct administration areas (with specific boundaries) in some countries.

province.description: A subdivision of a country similar to a state.

district.description: A district – a type of administrative division that, in some countries, is managed by local government.

county.description: A county - a geographical region of a country.

subdistrict.description: A subdistrict - a subdivision of a district used for administrative or other purposes.

municipality.description: A municipality - single urban administrative division having corporate status.

city.description: The largest urban settlement or settlements within the territory.

borough.description: A part in a larger city grouped into administrative unit.

suburb.description: A part of a town or city with a well-known name and often a distinct identity.

quarter.description: A quarter is a named, geographically localised place within a suburb of a larger city or within a town, which is bigger than a neighbourhood.

neighbourhood.description: A neighbourhood is a smaller named, geographically localised place within a suburb of a larger city or within a town or village

city_block.description: A named city block, usually surrounded by streets.

plot.description: A named plot is a tract or parcel of land owned or meant to be owned by some owner.

town.description: An important urban centre, between a village and a city in size.

village.description: A smaller distinct settlement, smaller than a town with few facilities available with people traveling to nearby towns to access these.

hamlet.description: A smaller rural community, typically with fewer than 100-200 inhabitants, and little infrastructure.

isolated_dwelling.description: The smallest kind of settlement (1-2 households).

allotments.description: A separate settlement, which is located outside an officially inhabited locality and has its own addressing

continent.description: One of the seven continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania, South America.

archipelago.description: A named group or chain of closely related islands and islets.

island.description: Any piece of land that is completely surrounded by water and isolated from other significant landmasses (bigger than 1 km²).

islet.description: A very small island (smaller than 1 km²).

square.description: A town or village square: a (typically) paved open space, generally of architectural significance, which is surrounded by buildings in a built-up area such as a city, town or village.

locality.description: A named place that has no population.

polder.description: A land area that forms an artificial hydrological entity enclosed by embankments and usually is under sea level.

sea.description: A large body of salt water part of, or connected to, an ocean.

ocean.description: The world's five main major oceanic divisions.

cable.description: An insulated cable carrying electrical power, such as transmission or distribution cables located underground and undersea cables

line.description: High-voltage power lines used for power transmission, usually supported by towers or pylons

minor_line.description: Minor power lines forming the distribution grid, usually carried by poles.

abandoned.description: The course of a former railway which has been abandoned and

disused.description: A section of railway which is no longer used but where the track and infrastructure remains in place. The track is likely overgrown with endemic vegetation, but could be brought back into useful service with only minor effort.

camp_pitch.description: A tent or caravan pitch location within a campsite or caravan site.

camp_site.description: An area where people can camp overnight using tents, camper vans, or caravans.

caravan_site.description: A place where you can stay in a caravan overnight or for longer periods.

picnic_site.description: A locality that is suitable for outdoors eating, with facilities to aid a picnic such as tables and benches.

river.description: The water covered area of a river.

oxbow.description: U-shaped body of water formed when a wide meander from the main stem of a river is cut off to create a lake.

canal.description: The area covered by the water of a canal

ditch.description: The water covered area of a waterway=ditch - a simple artificial waterway serving as a barrier or for draining surrounding land.

lock.description: Water area of the lock chamber.

lake.description: A natural or semi-natural body of relatively still fresh or salt water which is surrounded by land.

reservoir.description: An artificial lake which may be used to store water. Usually formed by a dam over a natural water course.

pond.description: A small body of standing water, man-made in most cases

basin.description: An area of land artificially graded to hold water. Includes also structures typically without water.

lagoon.description: A body of shallow sea water or brackish water separated from the sea by a barrier.

stream_pool.description: A stream pool: a small but relatively deep collection of fresh water, occurring along a narrow stream.

reflecting_pool.description: A shallow pool of water, undisturbed by fountain jets, for a calm reflective surface, often found in gardens, squares or at memorial sites

moat.description: A deep, wide defensive ditch, normally filled with water, dug to surround a fortified habitation.

river.description: The linear flow of a river, in flow direction.

riverbank.description: Using this tag is discouraged, use tag:natural=water + tag:water=river instead.

stream.description: A naturally-forming waterway that is too narrow to be classed as a river.

tidal_channel.description: A natural intertidal waterway in mangroves, salt marshes, and tidal flats with water flow in the direction of the tide.

canal.description: An artificial open flow waterway used to carry useful water for transportation, waterpower, or irrigation.

drain.description: An artificial free flow waterway used for carrying superfluous water, usually lined with concrete.

ditch.description: A small artificial drainage waterway, usually unlined.

fairway.description: A navigable route in a lake or sea, often marked by buoys or beacons.

dam.description: A barrier built across a river or stream to block and regulate the flow of the river.

weir.description: A barrier built across a river to control water speed and depth. Designed to let water flow over the top all the time or at least in specific conditions.

waterfall.description: A place where water flows over a vertical drop or a series of drops in the course of a stream or river.
